title: Mudroom Lockers – with CHARACTER!
summary: As we restore our historic home, we strive to make every detail look original to the home.
description: As we restore our historic home, we strive to make every detail look original to the home. So the arches and fluting on these mudroom lockers mirror other features in our home! We love the character it adds, AND the function it will bring to our family!

cutList.part: (6/4 Red Oak, Bench Seat) Seat long [Glue Up Panel] note: glue up is oversized
(6/4 Red Oak, Bench Seat) Seat long [Glue Up Panel] note: glue up is oversized
(4/4 Poplar, Bench Face Frame) Center Stile
(4/4 Poplar, Bench Face Frame) Adjoining Stile
(4/4 Poplar, Bench Face Frame) Lower Stile
(4/4 Poplar, Top Face Frame) Rail Arch [Glue Up Panel] note: glue up is oversized
(4/4 Poplar, Top Face Frame) Center Stile
(4/4 Poplar, Top Face Frame) Adjoining Stile Wide
(4/4 Poplar, Top Face Frame) Adjoining Stile Short
(4/4 Poplar, Top Trim) Crown Moulding, Pillars
(4/4 Poplar, Top Trim) Shelf Bull Nose

steps.title: Break down sheet goods
steps.body: Using a Track saw, cut down all the sheet goods.

steps.title: layout the dado's and grooves
steps.body: Layout and cut the dado's and grooves. we use a dado stack on the upper carcasses and a router on the bench carcasses.

steps.title: Assembly carcasses
steps.body:
Glue and assembly the carcasses.

**Tip:** Checking for square You will want to assembly the carcasses on a flat surface. It helps to assemble it on a square surface, regardless you should check for square any adjustments can be made by clamping at an angle pulling one side into square.

steps.title: Prep Rough stock
steps.body: Plane the rough lumber to desired thickness. This is really an ongoing process only planning the boards needed at the time.

steps.title: Glue up bench seats
steps.body: We prepped the edges for the bench seat and then, biscuit, glued and clamped the bench seats.

steps.title: Layout and cut face frames
steps.body: After planing the boards down to desired thickness, We laid out all the individual components on the board, cut them the width and length and then placed them where they per their assembly.

steps.title: Pocket holes
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the rails of the face frames.

steps.title: Assemble face frames
steps.body: Glue, clamp and screw together face frame.

steps.title: Glue the Face frame to the carcass
steps.body:
Glue the face of the carcass position and clamp the face frame to the carcass.

**Tip:** Keeping the face frame in place Use a hammer a few brad nails into the carcass and cut the heads off, prior to adding the face frame, this will keep it elevated so you can position it where you want. It will also keep it from sliding around while you clamp it.

steps.title: Glue up boards for the arches
steps.body: Glue up boards for the arches, cut to final dimension on a track saw or tablesaw. Then cut on a band saw and sand or use a template with a router/pattern bit to have consistent arches.

steps.title: Pocket hole the arches and rails
steps.body: Pocket hole arches and rails for upper lockers.

steps.title: Assemble face frame
steps.body: Join the face frames together.

steps.title: Glue face frames to upper lockers
steps.body:
Glue face frames to upper lockers, it seemed impossible to clamp the long stiles, so we drilled counterbores and screwed them to the carcass.

**Tip:** Bracing wide Stile I created square brackets for the adjoining stiles, the board are so wide and had a slight twist, so we the brackets gave them a little extra clamping surface. You can see them peaking in the upper right hand photo.

steps.title: Installing the lowers
steps.body: This is very similar to installing cabinets. Making sure the are level vertically and horizontally, shim and screw using cabinet screws.

steps.title: Install the uppers and trim out.
steps.body:
We provided 1:1 prints if printed on a Size C Sheet, you can use these as a template to cut out the upper arches, or you can draw them out and then use that as a template.

**Tip:** Fluting you can route the fluting using a radius template for a router or leave them plain, we opted to have the extra detail.

steps.title: Creating fluting with a palm router
steps.body: Using a palm router and a edge guide we routed the fluting into the tall columns.

steps.title: Trim out
steps.body: We glues and nailed the trim to the face frames.

steps.title: Add crown moulding
steps.body: We created the column crown moulding with the router and then glued and nailed them to the columns.

steps.title: Route the bullnose
steps.body: Using a Bullnose router bit we routed the face of a 7/8" board, then cut the pieces on the table saw.

steps.title: Shelf face details
steps.body: Glue and nail the shelf face details.

steps.title: All done!
steps.body: You're ready for prime an paint!
